name: Higuera de Albalat to Campillo de Deleitosa — circular via the Puerto de Campillo
name_original: Higuera de Albalat a Campillo de Deleitosa — circular por el Puerto de Campillo
region_intro: Road cycling around Higuera de Albalat is characterized by abrupt terrain, featuring the Sierra de la Caldilla, Sierra del Medio, and the Tagus River valley. This region, part of the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve, offers diverse landscapes with dehesa (holm oak and cork oak woodlands) and elevations reaching over 700 meters at points like the Puerto de Campillo. The area's geological significance is highlighted by its proximity to the Villuercas-Ibores-Jara Geopark, providing a challenging environment for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Higuera de Albalat The most popular road cycling route is Almaraz to Puerto de Campillo — circular via Puerto de Miravete and Deleitosa, a 40.2 miles (64.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 0 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant climbs and panoramic views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de Miravete Loop, a moderate 28.9 miles (46.5 km) path. This route offers varied terrain through the Sierra de Miravete, with ascents and descents. Local road cyclists also love the Deleitosa from Puerto de Miravete — circular by Higuera de Albalat, a 48.2 miles (77.6 km) trail leading through the Puerto de Miravete, often completed in about 4 hours 26 minutes. Road cycling around Higuera de Albalat is defined by abrupt terrain, dehesa landscapes, and the Tagus River valley. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Higuera de Albalat are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 150 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Higuera de Albalat's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Higuera de Albalat?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around Higuera de Albalat, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The region is known for its abrupt terrain and scenic landscapes.

Q: What is the terrain like for road cycling in Higuera de Albalat?
A: Road cycling around Higuera de Albalat is characterized by abrupt terrain, featuring the Sierra de la Caldilla, Sierra del Medio, and the Tagus River valley. You can expect diverse landscapes with dehesa (holm oak and cork oak woodlands) and elevations reaching over 700 meters at points like the Puerto de Campillo. The area's geological significance is highlighted by its proximity to the Villuercas-Ibores-Jara Geopark, providing a challenging environment for cyclists.

Q: Are there road cycling routes for all skill levels?
A: Yes, the routes in Higuera de Albalat cater to various abilities. While the region is known for its challenging climbs, there are 1 easy route, 12 moderate routes, and 6 difficult routes available, ensuring options for beginners to advanced cyclists.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while cycling?
A: You'll encounter stunning natural beauty, as Higuera de Albalat is nestled within the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve. Expect Mediterranean flora and fauna, dehesa landscapes with ancient holm and cork oaks like the impressive 'Los Cercones' Cork Oak, and the scenic Tagus River valley. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371591359/higuera-de-albalat-to-campillo-de-deleitosa-circular-via-the-puerto-de-campillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Higuera de Albalat to Campillo de Deleitosa — circular via the Puerto de Campillo&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, offers rewarding views from its summit.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can explore prehistoric settlements in the Sierra de la Caldilla, the remains of an 11th-century Muslim watchtower on Castil Oreja hill, and the Church of San Sebastián in town. Near Campillo de Deleitosa, you might pass the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5477194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acueducto de las Herrerías&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almaraz Bridge over the Tagus River&lt;/a&gt; also offers panoramic views.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Higuera de Albalat?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the challenging climbs, the stunning natural scenery of the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve, and the opportunities for cultural discovery along the routes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371626413/almaraz-to-puerto-de-campillo-circular-via-puerto-de-miravete-and-deleitosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almaraz to Puerto de Campillo — circular via Puerto de Miravete and Deleitosa&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 64.7 km with significant climbs and panoramic views.

Q: What are some challenging climbs for road cyclists in the area?
A: The region offers several challenging climbs. The ascent to the Puerto de Campillo is a notable one, reaching 700 meters above sea level and providing gratifying views. Routes like the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371596312/valdecanas-reservoir-loop-from-almaraz-via-puerto-de-campillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecañas Reservoir Loop from Almaraz via Puerto de Campillo&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or lakes?
A: Yes, some routes offer views of the region's reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371596312/valdecanas-reservoir-loop-from-almaraz-via-puerto-de-campillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecañas Reservoir Loop from Almaraz via Puerto de Campillo&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, providing scenic vistas of the Valdecañas reservoir. You can also find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5986707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Valdecañas reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Valdecañas Reservoir Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Higuera de Albalat?
A: Given its Mediterranean climate and natural features, spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for road biking in Higuera de Albalat. The temperatures are milder, and the landscapes are vibrant, making for a more comfortable and enjoyable cycling experience. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and wetter.

Q: Can I find routes that are part of larger cycling networks?
A: Yes, Higuera de Albalat is part of the Ciclosenda del Tajo (Tagus Cycle Route). This route combines trails and roads, allowing you to explore the untamed nature of the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve and observe its diverse birdlife along the Tagus River.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints worth visiting on a road bike?
A: Absolutely. The summit of the Puerto de Campillo, reached on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371591359/higuera-de-albalat-to-campillo-de-deleitosa-circular-via-the-puerto-de-campillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Higuera de Albalat to Campillo de Deleitosa — circular via the Puerto de Campillo&lt;/a&gt;, offers gratifying panoramic views. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696287" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almaraz Bridge over the Tagus River&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent scenic vistas.
tips: At the foot of the Sierra de la Caldilla, is the small historic town of Higuera. Stop by the charming San Sebastián church if you need to rest and grab a bite to eat.

Nice climb to Puerto de Campillo, 2.75 km coming from the south side, it is not too long. The views from the top at 700 meters above sea level are gratifying. Prepare for the descent towards Campillo de Deleitosa.

waypoints.name: Church of San Sebastián of Higuera de Albalat
